common.faq1Question: What can I do if I run out of credits?
common.faq1Answer:
If you exceed your credit limit, you have a few options:
1. Upgrade to a higher-tier plan with more credits.
2. Purchase additional credits to supplement your current plan.
3. Use your monthly free credits, which refresh automatically every month.

faqs.title: FAQs
faqs.q1: Can I change my subscription anytime?
faqs.a1: Yes. Both Monthly and Yearly users can update your subscription plan anytime. Yearly plans are eligible for prorated refunds based on remaining credits.
faqs.q2: What extra benefits do I get after subscribing?
faqs.a2: You'll get early access to new model integrations, exclusive features, premium templates, and special discounts.
faqs.q3: Can my team share one account?
faqs.a3: We recommend each member have their own login. Team and enterprise plans include shared workspaces and member management for better security and collaboration.
faqs.q4: What can I do if I run out of credits?
faqs.a4:
If you exceed your credit limit, you have a few options:
1. Upgrade to a higher-tier plan with more credits.
2. Purchase additional credits to supplement your current plan.
3. Use your monthly free credits, which refresh automatically every month.
faqs.q5: What happens when I upgrade my plan?
faqs.a5: When you upgrade, all benefits and credits from your new plan are unlocked immediately. Any unused credits from your previous plan will be prorated and automatically applied toward your next payment.
faqs.q6: Can unused credits roll over to next month?
faqs.a6: No, credits reset at the start of each billing cycle. Any unused credits from the previous month won't carry over, ensuring every user begins each month with a full allocation.

newUserGuide.step1Description: Everything starts with a raw idea. It holds the text prompt that guide your image generation

seo.title: OpenClaw x OpenCreator | AI agent skills for commercial content
seo.description: Connect OpenClaw to OpenCreator: install skills with one command, run workflow templates via API, and iterate in natural language.

hero.subtitle: Speak your content need in plain language. OpenClaw discovers workflow templates, runs OpenCreator APIs, and helps you iterate to a scalable commercial production lane.

howSection.title: How It Works
howSection.subtitle: From your first sentence to production-ready asset batches.

steps.title: Tell
steps.description: Describe what you want to create in plain language — your agent figures out the rest.

steps.title: Run
steps.description: OpenClaw selects a workflow template and executes it via the OpenCreator API.

steps.title: Refine
steps.description: Iterate through conversation — adjust style, parameters, or scale to batch production.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Common questions from the OpenClaw x OpenCreator manual.

items.question: Can I request detailed customization during generation?
items.answer: Yes. Ask OpenClaw to modify workflow structure, parameters, or prompts in natural language (this consumes tokens), or edit directly in OpenCreator frontend for more control without OpenClaw token overhead.

items.question: A run looked great. How do I reproduce it next time?
items.answer: Ask OpenClaw to review and memorize that successful run, especially rework issues and fixes. You can also request an updated skill markdown for manual review.

items.question: How can I save tokens?
items.answer: Use a cost-effective main model, request media outputs as links, and keep conversation context focused and concise.

items.question: OpenClaw sometimes forgets context. What should I do?
items.answer: This is common after switching main models. Stabilize on one model first, and if you switch, keep a backup of your skill file then let the new model review it before resuming.

items.question: Can I run my own workflows instead of template-market ones?
items.answer: Yes. Copy your Workflow ID from workflow file -> Share -> Copy Workflow ID, then send that ID to OpenClaw for direct execution.

items.question: How is API usage billed?
items.answer: API consumption follows your OpenCreator credit usage. Make sure your account has enough credits before running generation sessions.
